Command.Name: service
Command.Description: Service lifecycle management: start, stop, status.

Command.Name: start
Command.Description: Start the ExcelMCP Service if not already running.

Command.Name: stop
Command.Description: Gracefully stop the ExcelMCP Service.

Command.Name: status
Command.Description: Show service status (running, PID, sessions, uptime).

Command.Name: batch
Command.Description: Execute multiple commands from a JSON file or stdin. Outputs NDJSON (one result per line).

Option.Description: JSON file with command array. Use '-' for stdin (NDJSON, one command per line). If omitted, reads from stdin.
Default session ID for all commands. Overridden by per-command sessionId. Auto-captured from session.open/create if not set.
Stop execution on first error (default: continue all commands).

Command.Name: session
Command.Description: Session management. WORKFLOW: open -> use sessionId -> close (--save to persist).

Command.Name: create
Command.Description: Create a new Excel file, open it, and create a session.

Argument.Name: FILE
Argument.Description: Path to the new Excel file to create

Option.Description: Session timeout in seconds

Command.Name: open
Command.Description: Open an Excel file and create a session.

Argument.Name: FILE
Argument.Description: Path to the Excel file to open

Option.Description: Session timeout in seconds

Command.Name: close
Command.Description: Close a session. Use --save to persist changes.

Option.Description: Save changes before closing
Session ID to close

Command.Name: list
Command.Description: List active sessions.

Command.Name: save
Command.Description: Save a session without closing it.

Option.Description: Session ID to save

Command.Name: calculationmode
Command.Description: Control Excel recalculation (automatic vs manual). Set manual mode before bulk writes for faster performance, then recalculate once at the end.

Option.Description: Target calculation mode (required for: set-mode)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Range address (required for Range scope)
Scope: Workbook, Sheet, or Range (required for: calculate)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Sheet name (required for Sheet/Range scope)

Command.Name: chart
Command.Description: Chart lifecycle - create, read, move, and delete embedded charts. POSITIONING (choose one): - targetRange (PREFERRED): Cell range like 'F2:K15' — positions chart within cells, no point math needed. - left/top: Manual positioning in points (72 points = 1 inch). - Neither: Auto-positions chart below all existing content (used range + other charts). COLLISION DETECTION: All create/move/fit-to-range operations automatically check for overlaps with data and other charts. Warnings are returned in the result message if collisions are detected. Always verify layout with screenshot(capture-sheet) after creating charts. CHART TYPES: 70+ types available including Column, Line, Pie, Bar, Area, XY Scatter. CREATE OPTIONS: - create-from-range: Create from cell range (e.g., 'A1:D10') - create-from-table: Create from Excel Table (uses table's data range) - create-from-pivottable: Create linked PivotChart Use chartconfig for series, titles, legends, styles, placement mode.

Option.Description: Name of the chart (or shape name) (required for: read, delete, move, fit-to-range)
Type of chart to create (required for: create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-pivottable)
Chart height in points
Left position in points from worksheet edge
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the source PivotTable (required for: create-from-pivottable)
Range to fit the chart to (e.g., A1:D10) (required for: fit-to-range)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Target worksheet name (required for: create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-pivottable, fit-to-range)
Data range for the chart (e.g., A1:D10) (required for: create-from-range)
Name of the Excel Table (required for: create-from-table)
Cell range to position chart within (e.g., 'F2:K15'). PREFERRED over left/top. When set, left/top are ignored.
Top position in points from worksheet edge
Chart width in points

Command.Name: chartconfig
Command.Description: Chart configuration - data source, series, type, title, axis labels, legend, and styling. SERIES MANAGEMENT: - add-series: Add data series with valuesRange (required) and optional categoryRange - remove-series: Remove series by 1-based index - set-source-range: Replace entire chart data source TITLES AND LABELS: - set-title: Set chart title (empty string hides title) - set-axis-title: Set axis labels (Category, Value, CategorySecondary, ValueSecondary) CHART STYLES: 1-48 (built-in Excel styles with different color schemes) DATA LABELS: Show values, percentages, series/category names. Positions: Center, InsideEnd, InsideBase, OutsideEnd, BestFit. TRENDLINES: Linear, Exponential, Logarithmic, Polynomial (order 2-6), Power, MovingAverage. PLACEMENT MODE: - 1: Move and size with cells - 2: Move but don't size with cells - 3: Don't move or size with cells (free floating) Use chart for lifecycle operations (create, delete, move, fit-to-range).

Option.Description: Which axis to set title for (Category, Value, SeriesAxis) (required for: set-axis-title, get-axis-number-format, set-axis-number-format, get-axis-scale, set-axis-scale, set-gridlines)
Periods to extend backward
Optional range for category labels (e.g., A2:A10)
Name of the chart (required)
New chart type to apply (required for: set-chart-type)
Display trendline equation on chart
Display R-squared value on chart
Periods to extend forward
Force trendline through specific Y-intercept
Invert colors for negative values
Position of data labels relative to data points
Optional position for the legend
Major gridline interval (null for auto)
Marker fill color (#RRGGBB)
Marker border color (#RRGGBB)
Marker size in points (2-72)
Marker shape style
Maximum axis value (null for auto)
Minimum axis value (null for auto)
Minor gridline interval (null for auto)
Custom name for the trendline
Excel number format code (e.g., "$#,##0", "0.00%") (required for: set-axis-number-format)
Polynomial order (2-6, for Polynomial type)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Moving average period (for MovingAverage type)
Placement mode: 1=MoveAndSize, 2=Move, 3=FreeFloating (required for: set-placement)
Separator string between label components
1-based index of the series to remove (required for: remove-series, set-series-format, list-trendlines, add-trendline, delete-trendline, set-trendline)
Display name for the series (required for: add-series)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Show bubble size (bubble charts)
Show category name on labels
Show major gridlines (null to keep current)
Show minor gridlines (null to keep current)
Show percentage values. Only meaningful for pie and doughnut chart types; setting to true on other chart types has no visual effect.
Show series name on labels
Show data values on labels
New data source range (e.g., Sheet1!A1:D10) (required for: set-source-range)
Excel chart style ID (1-48 for most chart types) (required for: set-style)
Title text to display (required for: set-title, set-axis-title)
1-based index of the trendline to delete (required for: delete-trendline, set-trendline)
Type of trendline (Linear, Exponential, etc.) (required for: add-trendline)
Range containing series values (e.g., B2:B10) (required for: add-series)
True to show legend, false to hide (required for: show-legend)

Command.Name: conditionalformat
Command.Description: Conditional formatting - visual rules based on cell values. TYPES: cellValue (requires operatorType+formula1), expression (formula only). Both camelCase and kebab-case accepted. FORMAT: interiorColor/fontColor as #RRGGBB, fontBold/Italic, borderStyle/Color. OPERATORS: equal, notEqual, greater, less, greaterEqual, lessEqual, between, notBetween. For 'between' and 'notBetween', both formula1 and formula2 are required.

Option.Description: Border color (#RRGGBB or color index)
Border style: none, continuous, dash, dot, etc.
Bold font
Font color (#RRGGBB or color index)
Italic font
First formula/value for condition
Second formula/value (for between/notBetween)
Fill color (#RRGGBB or color index)
Interior pattern (1=Solid, -4142=None, 9=Gray50, etc.)
XlFormatConditionOperator: equal, notEqual, greater, less, greaterEqual, lessEqual, between, notBetween
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Range address (A1 notation or named range) (required)

Rule type: cellValue (or cell-value), expression, colorScale, dataBar, top10, iconSet, uniqueValues, blanksCondition, timePeriod, aboveAverage. Both camelCase and kebab-case accepted. (required for: add-rule)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Sheet name (empty for active sheet) (required)

Command.Name: connection
Command.Description: Data connections (OLEDB, ODBC, ODC import). TEXT/WEB/CSV: Use powerquery instead. Power Query connections auto-redirect to powerquery. TIMEOUT: 30 min auto-timeout for refresh/load-to.

Option.Description: Run query in background (null to keep current)
SQL query or table name
Name of the connection to view (required for: view, create, refresh, delete, load-to, get-properties, set-properties, test)
OLEDB or ODBC connection string (required for: create)
Optional description for the connection
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Refresh when file opens (null to keep current)
Auto-refresh interval in minutes (null to keep current)
Save password in connection (null to keep current)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Target worksheet name (required for: load-to)
Optional timeout for the refresh operation

Command.Name: datamodel
Command.Description: Data Model (Power Pivot) - DAX measures and table management. CRITICAL: WORKSHEET TABLES AND DATA MODEL ARE SEPARATE! - After table append changes, Data Model still has OLD data - MUST call refresh to sync changes - Power Query refresh auto-syncs (no manual refresh needed) PREREQUISITE: Tables must be added to the Data Model first. Use table add-to-datamodel for worksheet tables, or powerquery to import and load data directly to the Data Model. DAX MEASURES: - Create with DAX formulas like 'SUM(Sales[[Amount]])' - DAX formulas are auto-formatted on CREATE/UPDATE via Dax.Formatter (SQLBI) - Read operations return raw DAX as stored DAX EVALUATE QUERIES: - Use evaluate to execute DAX EVALUATE queries against the Data Model - Returns tabular results from queries like 'EVALUATE TableName' - Supports complex DAX: SUMMARIZE, FILTER, CALCULATETABLE, TOPN, etc. DMV (DYNAMIC MANAGEMENT VIEW) QUERIES: - Use execute-dmv to query Data Model metadata via SQL-like syntax - Syntax: SELECT * FROM $SYSTEM.SchemaRowset (ONLY SELECT * supported) - Use DISCOVER_SCHEMA_ROWSETS to list all available DMVs Use datamodelrel for relationships between tables.

Option.Description: DAX formula for the measure (will be auto-formatted) (required for: create-measure)
Path to file containing daxFormula
DAX EVALUATE query (e.g., "EVALUATE 'TableName'" or "EVALUATE SUMMARIZE(...)") (required for: evaluate)
Path to file containing daxQuery
Optional: Description of the measure
DMV query in SQL-like syntax (e.g., "SELECT * FROM $SYSTEM.TMSCHEMA_TABLES") (required for: execute-dmv)
Path to file containing dmvQuery
Optional: Format type (Currency, Decimal, Percentage, General)
Name of the measure to get (required for: read, delete-measure, create-measure, update-measure)
New name for the table (required for: rename-table)
Current name of the table (required for: rename-table)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the table to list columns from (required for: list-columns, read-table, delete-table, create-measure)
Optional: Timeout for the refresh operation

Command.Name: datamodelrelationship
Command.Description: Data Model relationships - link tables for cross-table DAX calculations. CRITICAL: Deleting or recreating tables removes ALL their relationships. Use list-relationships before table operations to backup, then recreate relationships after schema changes. RELATIONSHIP REQUIREMENTS: - Both tables must exist in the Data Model first - Columns must have compatible data types - fromTable/fromColumn = many-side (detail table, foreign key) - toTable/toColumn = one-side (lookup table, primary key) ACTIVE VS INACTIVE: - Only ONE active relationship can exist between two tables - Use active=false when creating alternative paths - DAX USERELATIONSHIP() activates inactive relationships

Option.Description: Whether the relationship should be active (default: true) (required for: update-relationship)
Source column name (required for: read-relationship, create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)
Source table name (required for: read-relationship, create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Session ID from 'session open' command
Target column name (required for: read-relationship, create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)
Target table name (required for: read-relationship, create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)

Command.Name: diag
Command.Description: Diagnostic commands for testing CLI/MCP infrastructure without Excel. These commands validate parameter parsing, routing, JSON serialization, and error handling — no Excel COM session needed.

Option.Description: Required integer parameter (required for: validate-params)
Optional label parameter
The message to echo back (required) (required for: echo)
Required name parameter (required for: validate-params)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Optional tag to include in the response
Optional boolean flag (default: false)

Command.Name: namedrange
Command.Description: Named ranges for formulas/parameters. CREATE/UPDATE: value is cell reference (e.g., 'Sheet1!$A$1'). WRITE: value is data to store. TIP: range(rangeAddress=namedRangeName) for bulk data read/write.

Option.Description: Name of the named range (required for: write, read, update, create, delete)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
New cell reference (e.g., Sheet1!$A$1:$B$10) (required for: update, create)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Value to set (required for: write)

Command.Name: pivottable
Command.Description: PivotTable lifecycle management: create from various sources, list, read details, refresh, and delete. Use pivottablefield for field operations, pivottablecalc for calculated fields and layout. BEST PRACTICE: Use 'list' before creating. Prefer 'refresh' or field modifications over delete+recreate. Delete+recreate loses field configurations, filters, sorting, and custom layouts. REFRESH: Call 'refresh' after configuring fields with pivottablefield to update the visual display. This is especially important for OLAP/Data Model PivotTables where field operations are structural only and don't automatically trigger a visual refresh. CREATE OPTIONS: - 'create-from-range': Use source sheet and range address for data range - 'create-from-table': Use an Excel Table (ListObject) as source - 'create-from-datamodel': Use a Power Pivot Data Model table as source

Option.Description: Destination cell address (e.g., "A1") (required for: create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-datamodel)
Destination worksheet name (required for: create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-datamodel)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the PivotTable (required for: read, create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-datamodel, delete, refresh)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Source range address (e.g., "A1:F100") (required for: create-from-range)
Source worksheet name (required for: create-from-range)
Name of the Excel Table (required for: create-from-table, create-from-datamodel)
Optional timeout for the refresh operation

Command.Name: pivottablecalc
Command.Description: PivotTable calculated fields/members, layout configuration, and data extraction. Use pivottable for lifecycle, pivottablefield for field placement. CALCULATED FIELDS (for regular PivotTables): - Create custom fields using formulas like '=Revenue-Cost' or '=Quantity*UnitPrice' - Can reference existing fields by name - After creating, use pivottablefield add-value-field to add to Values area - For complex multi-table calculations, prefer DAX measures with datamodel CALCULATED MEMBERS (for OLAP/Data Model PivotTables only): - Create using MDX expressions - Member types: Member, Set, Measure LAYOUT OPTIONS: - 0 = Compact (default, fields in single column) - 1 = Tabular (each field in separate column - best for export/analysis) - 2 = Outline (hierarchical with expand/collapse)

Option.Description: Display folder path for organizing measures (optional)
Name for the calculated field (required for: create-calculated-field, delete-calculated-field, set-subtotals)
Formula using field references (e.g., "=Revenue-Cost") (required for: create-calculated-field, create-calculated-member)
Name for the calculated member (MDX naming format) (required for: create-calculated-member, delete-calculated-member)
Number format code for the calculated member (optional)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the PivotTable (required)
Layout form: 0=Compact, 1=Tabular, 2=Outline (required for: set-layout)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Show column grand totals (right summary column) (required for: set-grand-totals)
Show row grand totals (bottom summary row) (required for: set-grand-totals)
True to show automatic subtotals, false to hide (required for: set-subtotals)
Solve order for calculation precedence (default: 0)
Type of calculated member (Member, Set, or Measure)

Command.Name: pivottablefield
Command.Description: PivotTable field management: add/remove/configure fields, filtering, sorting, and grouping. Use pivottable for lifecycle, pivottablecalc for calculated fields and layout. IMPORTANT: Field operations modify structure only. Call pivottable refresh after configuring fields to update the visual display, especially for OLAP/Data Model PivotTables. FIELD AREAS: - Row fields: Group data by categories (add-row-field) - Column fields: Create column headers (add-column-field) - Value fields: Aggregate numeric data with Sum, Count, Average, etc. (add-value-field) - Filter fields: Add report-level filters (add-filter-field) AGGREGATION FUNCTIONS: Sum, Count, Average, Max, Min, Product, CountNumbers, StdDev, StdDevP, Var, VarP GROUPING: - Date fields: Group by Days, Months, Quarters, Years (group-by-date) - Numeric fields: Group by ranges with start/end/interval (group-by-numeric) NUMBER FORMAT: Use US format codes like '#,##0.00' for currency or '0.00%' for percentages.

Option.Description: Aggregation function (for Regular and OLAP auto-create mode) (required for: set-field-function)
Optional custom name for the field/measure (required for: set-field-name)
Sort direction
Ending value (null = use field maximum)

Name of the field to add (required for: add-row-field, add-column-field, add-value-field, add-filter-field, remove-field, set-field-function, set-field-name, set-field-format, set-field-filter, sort-field, group-by-date, group-by-numeric)
Grouping interval (Months, Quarters, Years) (required for: group-by-date)
Size of each group (e.g., 100 for groups of 100) (required for: group-by-numeric)
Number format string (required for: set-field-format)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the PivotTable (required)
Optional position in row area (1-based)
Values to show (others will be hidden) (required for: set-field-filter) (JSON format)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Starting value (null = use field minimum)

Command.Name: powerquery
Command.Description: Power Query M code and data loading. TEST-FIRST DEVELOPMENT WORKFLOW (BEST PRACTICE): 1. evaluate - Test M code WITHOUT persisting (catches syntax errors, validates sources, shows data preview) 2. create/update - Store VALIDATED query in workbook 3. refresh/load-to - Load data to destination Skip evaluate only for trivial literal tables. IF CREATE/UPDATE FAILS: Use evaluate to get the actual M engine error message, fix code, retry. DATETIME COLUMNS: Always include Table.TransformColumnTypes() in M code to set column types explicitly. Without explicit types, dates may be stored as numbers and Data Model relationships may fail. DESTINATIONS: 'worksheet' (default), 'data-model' (for DAX), 'both', 'connection-only'. Use 'data-model' to load to Power Pivot, then use datamodel to create DAX measures. TARGET CELL: targetCellAddress places tables without clearing sheet. TIMEOUT: 30 min auto-timeout for refresh and load-to. For quick queries, use timeout=60 or similar. timeout=0 or omitted uses the 30 min default.

Option.Description: Load destination mode (required for: load-to)
Raw M code (inline string) (required for: create, update, evaluate)
Path to file containing mCode
New name for the query (required for: rename)
Current name of the query (required for: rename)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the query to view (required for: view, refresh, get-load-config, delete, create, update, load-to, unload)
Whether to refresh data after update (default: true)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Optional target cell address for worksheet loads (e.g., "B5"). Required when loading to an existing worksheet with other data.
Target worksheet name (required for LoadToTable and LoadToBoth; defaults to query name when omitted)
Maximum time to wait for refresh (required for: refresh)

Command.Name: range
Command.Description: Core range operations: get/set values and formulas, copy ranges, clear content, and discover data regions. Use rangeedit for insert/delete/find/sort. Use rangeformat for styling/validation. Use rangelink for hyperlinks and cell protection. Calculation mode and explicit recalculation are handled by calculationmode. BEST PRACTICE: Use 'get-values' to check existing data before overwriting. Use 'clear-contents' (not 'clear-all') to preserve cell formatting when clearing data. set-values preserves existing formatting; use set-number-format after if format change needed. DATA FORMAT: values and formulas are 2D JSON arrays representing rows and columns. Example: [[[[row1col1, row1col2]], [[row2col1, row2col2]]]] Single cell returns [[[[value]]]] (always 2D). REQUIRED PARAMETERS: - sheetName + rangeAddress for cell operations (e.g., sheetName='Sheet1', rangeAddress='A1:D10') - For named ranges, use sheetName='' (empty string) and rangeAddress='MyNamedRange' COPY OPERATIONS: Specify source and target sheet/range for copy operations. NUMBER FORMATS: Use US locale format codes (e.g., '#,##0.00', 'mm/dd/yyyy', '0.00%').

Option.Description: Single cell address (e.g., 'B5') - expands to contiguous data region around this cell (required for: get-current-region)
Number format code in US locale (e.g., '#,##0.00' for numbers, 'mm/dd/yyyy' for dates, '0.00%' for percentages, 'General' for default, '@' for text) (required for: set-number-format)
2D array of format codes - same dimensions as target range (e.g., [[[['#,##0.00', '0.00%']], [['mm/dd/yyyy', 'General']]]]). Optional if formatsFile is provided. (JSON format)
Path to a JSON file containing 2D array of format codes. Alternative to inline formats parameter.
2D array of formulas to set - include '=' prefix (e.g., [[[['=A1+B1', '=SUM(A:A)']], [['=C1*2', '=AVERAGE(B:B)']]]]). Optional if formulasFile is provided. (JSON format)
Path to a JSON file containing the formulas as a 2D array. Alternative to inline formulas parameter.
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.

Cell range address (e.g., 'A1', 'A1:D10', 'B:D') or named range name (e.g., 'SalesData') (required for: get-values, set-values, get-formulas, set-formulas, clear-all, clear-contents, clear-formats, get-number-formats, set-number-format, set-number-formats, get-info)
Session ID from 'session open' command

Name of the worksheet containing the range - REQUIRED for cell addresses, use empty string for named ranges only (required for: get-values, set-values, get-formulas, set-formulas, clear-all, clear-contents, clear-formats, get-number-formats, set-number-format, set-number-formats, get-used-range, get-current-region, get-info)
Source range address for copy operations (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required for: copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)
Source worksheet name for copy operations (required for: copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)
Target range address - can be single cell for paste destination (e.g., 'A1') (required for: copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)
Target worksheet name for copy operations (required for: copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)
2D array of values to set - rows are outer array, columns are inner array (e.g., [[[[1,2,3]],[[4,5,6]]]] for 2 rows x 3 cols). Optional if valuesFile is provided. (JSON format)
Path to a JSON or CSV file containing the values. JSON: 2D array. CSV: rows/columns. Alternative to inline values parameter.

Command.Name: rangeedit
Command.Description: Range editing operations: insert/delete cells, rows, and columns; find/replace text; sort data. Use range for values/formulas/copy/clear operations. INSERT/DELETE CELLS: Specify shift direction to control how surrounding cells move. - Insert: 'Down' or 'Right' - Delete: 'Up' or 'Left' INSERT/DELETE ROWS: Use row range like '5:10' to insert/delete rows 5-10. INSERT/DELETE COLUMNS: Use column range like 'B:D' to insert/delete columns B-D. FIND/REPLACE: Search within the specified range with optional case/cell matching. - Find returns up to 10 matching cell addresses with total count. - Replace modifies all matches by default. SORT: Specify sortColumns as array of {columnIndex: 1, ascending: true} objects. Column indices are 1-based relative to the range.

Option.Description: Direction to shift remaining cells: 'Up' or 'Left' (required for: delete-cells)
Search options: matchCase (default: false), matchEntireCell (default: false), searchFormulas (default: true) (required for: find)
Text or value to search for (required for: replace)
Whether the range has a header row to exclude from sorting (default: true)
Direction to shift existing cells: 'Down' or 'Right' (required for: insert-cells)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Cell range address where cells will be inserted (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required)
Replace options: matchCase (default: false), matchEntireCell (default: false), replaceAll (default: true) (required for: replace)
Text or value to replace matches with (required for: replace)
Text or value to search for (required for: find)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the worksheet containing the range (required)
Array of sort specifications: [[{columnIndex: 1, ascending: true}, ...]] - columnIndex is 1-based relative to range (required for: sort) (JSON format)

Command.Name: rangeformat
Command.Description: Range formatting operations: apply styles, set fonts/colors/borders, add data validation, merge cells, auto-fit dimensions. Use range tool for values/formulas/copy/clear operations. set-style: Apply a named Excel style (Heading 1, Good, Bad, Neutral, Normal). Best for semantic status labels (Good/Bad/Neutral have fill colours and are theme-aware) and document hierarchy (Heading 1/2/3). NOTE: Heading styles do NOT apply a fill colour — use format-range when you need a coloured header row. format-range: Apply any combination of bold, fillColor, fontColor, alignment, borders. Required whenever you need a fill colour or custom branding. Pass ALL desired properties in a SINGLE call — do not call format-range multiple times for the same range. COLORS: Hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., '#FF0000' for red, '#00FF00' for green) FONT: size in points (e.g., 12, 14, 16), alignment: 'left', 'center', 'right' / 'top', 'middle', 'bottom' DATA VALIDATION: Restrict cell input with validation rules: - Types: 'list', 'whole', 'decimal', 'date', 'time', 'textLength', 'custom' - For list validation, formula1 is the list source (e.g., '=$A$1:$A$10' or '"Option1,Option2,Option3"') - Operators: 'between', 'notBetween', 'equal', 'notEqual', 'greaterThan', 'lessThan', 'greaterThanOrEqual', 'lessThanOrEqual' MERGE: Combines cells into one. Only top-left cell value is preserved.

Option.Description: Whether to apply bold formatting
Border color as hex '#RRGGBB'
Border line style: 'continuous', 'dash', 'dot', 'dashdot', 'dashdotdot', 'double', 'slantdashdot', 'none'
Border weight: 'hairline', 'thin', 'medium', 'thick'
Width in points (1 point = 1/72 inch, approx 0.35mm). Standard width ~8.43 points. Range: 0.25-409 points. (required for: set-column-width)
Text for the error alert popup
Error alert style: 'stop' (prevents entry), 'warning' (allows override), 'information' (allows entry)
Title for the error alert popup
Cell fill (background) color as hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., '#FFFF00' for yellow)
Font (foreground) color as hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., '#FF0000' for red)
Font family name (e.g., 'Arial', 'Calibri', 'Times New Roman')
Font size in points (e.g., 10, 11, 12, 14, 16)
First validation formula/value - for list validation use range '=$A$1:$A$10' or inline '"A,B,C"'
Second validation formula/value - required only for 'between' and 'notBetween' operators
Horizontal text alignment: 'left', 'center', 'right', 'justify', 'fill'
Whether to allow blank cells in validation (default: true)
Text for the input message popup
Title for the input message popup
Whether to apply italic formatting
Text rotation in degrees (-90 to 90, or 255 for vertical)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Cell range address (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required)
Height in points (1 point = 1/72 inch, approx 0.35mm). Default row height ~15 points. Range: 0-409 points. (required for: set-row-height)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the worksheet containing the range (required)
Whether to show dropdown arrow for list validation (default: true)
Whether to show error alert on invalid input (default: true)
Whether to show input message when cell is selected (default: false)
Built-in or custom style name (e.g., 'Heading 1', 'Good', 'Bad', 'Currency', 'Percent'). Use 'Normal' to reset. (required for: set-style)
Whether to apply underline formatting
Validation comparison operator: 'between', 'notBetween', 'equal', 'notEqual', 'greaterThan', 'lessThan', 'greaterThanOrEqual', 'lessThanOrEqual'
Data validation type: 'list', 'whole', 'decimal', 'date', 'time', 'textLength', 'custom' (required for: validate-range)
Vertical text alignment: 'top', 'center' (or 'middle'), 'bottom', 'justify'
Whether to wrap text within cells

Command.Name: rangelink
Command.Description: Hyperlink and cell protection operations for Excel ranges. Use range for values/formulas, rangeformat for styling. HYPERLINKS: - 'add-hyperlink': Add a clickable hyperlink to a cell (URL can be web, file, or mailto) - 'remove-hyperlink': Remove hyperlink(s) from cells while keeping the cell content - 'list-hyperlinks': Get all hyperlinks on a worksheet - 'get-hyperlink': Get hyperlink details for a specific cell CELL PROTECTION: - 'set-cell-lock': Lock or unlock cells (only effective when sheet protection is enabled) - 'get-cell-lock': Check if cells are locked Note: Cell locking only takes effect when the worksheet is protected.

Option.Description: Single cell address (e.g., 'A1') (required for: add-hyperlink, get-hyperlink)
Text to display in the cell (optional, defaults to URL)
Lock status: true = locked (protected when sheet protection enabled), false = unlocked (editable) (required for: set-cell-lock)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Cell range address to remove hyperlinks from (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required for: remove-hyperlink, set-cell-lock, get-cell-lock)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the worksheet (required)
Tooltip text shown on hover (optional)
Hyperlink URL (web: 'https://...', file: 'file:///...', email: 'mailto:...') (required for: add-hyperlink)

Command.Name: screenshot
Command.Description: Capture Excel worksheet content as images for visual verification. Uses Excel's built-in rendering (CopyPicture) to capture ranges as PNG images. Captures formatting, conditional formatting, charts, and all visual elements. ACTIONS: - capture: Capture a specific range as an image - capture-sheet: Capture the entire used area of a worksheet RETURNS: Base64-encoded image data with dimensions metadata. For MCP: returned as native ImageContent (no file handling needed). For CLI: use --output <path> to save the image directly to a PNG/JPEG file instead of returning base64 inline. Quality defaults to Medium (JPEG 75% scale) which is 4-8x smaller than High (PNG). Use High only when fine detail inspection is needed.

Option.Description: Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Image quality: Medium (default, JPEG 75% scale), High (PNG full scale), Low (JPEG 50% scale)
Range to capture (e.g., "A1:F20")
Session ID from 'session open' command
Worksheet name (null for active sheet)

Command.Name: sheet
Command.Description: Worksheet lifecycle management: create, rename, copy, delete, move, list sheets. Use range for data operations. Use sheetstyle for tab colors and visibility. ATOMIC OPERATIONS: 'copy-to-file' and 'move-to-file' don't require a session - they open/close files automatically. POSITIONING: For 'move', 'copy-to-file', 'move-to-file' - use 'before' OR 'after' (not both) to position the sheet relative to another. If neither specified, moves to end. NOTE: MCP tool is manually implemented in ExcelWorksheetTool.cs to properly handle mixed session requirements (copy-to-file and move-to-file are atomic and don't need sessions).

Option.Description: Optional: Name of sheet to position after
Optional: Name of sheet to position before
Optional file path when batch contains multiple workbooks. If omitted, uses primary workbook.
New name for the worksheet (required for: rename)
Current name of the worksheet (required for: rename)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name for the new worksheet (required for: create, delete, move)
Full path to the source workbook (required for: copy-to-file, move-to-file)
Name of the source worksheet (required for: copy)
Name of the sheet to copy (required for: copy-to-file, move-to-file)
Full path to the target workbook (required for: copy-to-file, move-to-file)
Name for the copied worksheet (required for: copy)
Optional: New name for the copied sheet (default: keeps original name)

Command.Name: slicer
Command.Description: Slicer visual filters for PivotTables and Excel Tables. PIVOTTABLE SLICERS: create-slicer, list-slicers, set-slicer-selection, delete-slicer. TABLE SLICERS: create-table-slicer, list-table-slicers, set-table-slicer-selection, delete-table-slicer. NAMING: Auto-generate descriptive names like {FieldName}Slicer (e.g., RegionSlicer). SELECTION: selectedItems as list of strings. Empty list clears filter (shows all items). Set clearFirst=false to add to existing selection.

Option.Description: If true, clears existing selection before setting new items (default: true)
Name of the column to use for the slicer (required for: create-table-slicer)
Worksheet where slicer will be placed (required for: create-slicer, create-table-slicer)
Name of the field to use for the slicer (required for: create-slicer)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Name of the PivotTable to create slicer for (required for: create-slicer)
Top-left cell position for the slicer (e.g., "H2") (required for: create-slicer, create-table-slicer)
Items to select (show in PivotTable) (required for: set-slicer-selection, set-table-slicer-selection) (JSON format)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name for the new slicer (required for: create-slicer, set-slicer-selection, delete-slicer, create-table-slicer, set-table-slicer-selection, delete-table-slicer)
Name of the Excel Table (required for: create-table-slicer)

Command.Name: table
Command.Description: Excel Tables (ListObjects) - lifecycle and data operations. Tables provide structured references, automatic formatting, and Data Model integration. BEST PRACTICE: Use 'list' to check existing tables before creating. Prefer 'append'/'resize'/'rename' over delete+recreate to preserve references. WARNING: Deleting tables used as PivotTable sources or in Data Model relationships will break those objects. DATA MODEL WORKFLOW: To analyze worksheet data with DAX/Power Pivot: 1. Create or identify an Excel Table on a worksheet 2. Use 'add-to-datamodel' to add the table to Power Pivot 3. Then use datamodel to create DAX measures on it DAX-BACKED TABLES: Create tables populated by DAX EVALUATE queries: - 'create-from-dax': Create a new table backed by a DAX query (e.g., SUMMARIZE, FILTER) - 'update-dax': Update the DAX query for an existing DAX-backed table - 'get-dax': Get the DAX query info for a table (check if it's DAX-backed) Related: tablecolumn (filter/sort/columns), datamodel (DAX measures, evaluate queries)

Option.Description: Name of the column to set total function on (required for: set-column-total)
DAX EVALUATE query (e.g., 'EVALUATE Sales' or 'EVALUATE SUMMARIZE(...)') (required for: create-from-dax, update-dax)
True if first row contains column headers (default: true)
New name for the table (must be unique in workbook) (required for: rename)
New range address (e.g., 'A1:F20') (required for: resize)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Cell range address for the table (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required for: create)
2D array of row data to append - column order must match table columns. Optional if rowsFile is provided. (JSON format)
Path to a JSON or CSV file containing the rows to append. JSON: 2D array. CSV: rows/columns. Alternative to inline rows parameter.
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the worksheet to create the table on (required for: create, create-from-dax)
True to show totals row, false to hide (required for: toggle-totals)
When true, renames source table columns that contain literal bracket characters (removes brackets) before adding to the Data Model. This modifies the Excel table column headers in the worksheet.

Name for the new table (must be unique in workbook) (required for: create, rename, delete, read, resize, toggle-totals, set-column-total, append, get-data, set-style, add-to-data-model, create-from-dax, update-dax, get-dax)
Table style name (e.g., 'TableStyleMedium2', 'TableStyleLight1'). Optional. (required for: set-style)
Target cell address for table placement (default: 'A1')
Totals function name: Sum, Count, Average, Min, Max, CountNums, StdDev, Var, None (required for: set-column-total)
True to return only visible (non-filtered) rows; false for all rows (default: false)

Command.Name: tablecolumn
Command.Description: Table column, filtering, and sorting operations for Excel Tables (ListObjects). Use table for table-level lifecycle and data operations. FILTERING: - 'apply-filter': Simple criteria filter (e.g., ">100", "=Active", "<>Closed") - 'apply-filter-values': Filter by exact values (provide list of values to include) - 'clear-filters': Remove all active filters - 'get-filters': See current filter state SORTING: - 'sort': Single column sort (ascending/descending) - 'sort-multi': Multi-column sort (provide list of {columnName, ascending} objects) COLUMN MANAGEMENT: - 'add-column'/'remove-column'/'rename-column': Modify table structure NUMBER FORMATS: Use US locale format codes (e.g., '#,##0.00', '0%', 'yyyy-mm-dd')

Option.Description: Sort order: true = ascending (A-Z, 0-9), false = descending (default: true)
Name of the column to filter (required for: apply-filter, apply-filter-values, add-column, remove-column, sort, get-column-number-format, set-column-number-format)
Filter criteria string (e.g., '>100', '=Active', '<>Closed') (required for: apply-filter)
Number format code in US locale (e.g., '#,##0.00', '0%', 'yyyy-mm-dd') (required for: set-column-number-format)
New column name (required for: rename-column)
Current column name (required for: rename-column)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
1-based column position (optional, defaults to end of table)
Table region: 'Data', 'Headers', 'Totals', or 'All' (required for: get-structured-reference)
Session ID from 'session open' command
List of sort specifications: [[{columnName: 'Col1', ascending: true}, ...]] - applied in order (required for: sort-multi) (JSON format)
Name of the Excel table (required)
List of exact values to include in the filter (required for: apply-filter-values) (JSON format)

Command.Name: vba
Command.Description: VBA scripts (requires .xlsm and VBA trust enabled). PREREQUISITES: - Workbook must be macro-enabled (.xlsm) - VBA trust must be enabled for automation RUN: procedureName format is 'Module.Procedure' (e.g., 'Module1.MySub').

Option.Description: Name of the VBA module (required for: view, import, update, delete)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Optional parameters to pass to the procedure (required for: run)
Name of the procedure to run (e.g., "Module1.MySub") (required for: run)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Optional timeout for execution
VBA code to import (required for: import, update)
Path to file containing vbaCode

Command.Name: window
Command.Description: Control Excel window visibility, position, state, and status bar. Use to show/hide Excel, bring it to front, reposition, or maximize/minimize. Set status bar text to give users real-time feedback during operations. VISIBILITY: 'show' makes Excel visible AND brings to front. 'hide' hides Excel. Visibility changes are reflected in session metadata (session list shows updated state). WINDOW STATE values: 'normal', 'minimized', 'maximized'. ARRANGE presets: 'left-half', 'right-half', 'top-half', 'bottom-half', 'center', 'full-screen'. STATUS BAR: 'set-status-bar' displays text in Excel's status bar. 'clear-status-bar' restores default.

Option.Description: Window height in points
Window left position in points
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Preset name: 'left-half', 'right-half', 'top-half', 'bottom-half', 'center', 'full-screen' (required for: arrange)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Status bar text to display (e.g. "Building PivotTable from Sales data...") (required for: set-status-bar)
Window top position in points
Window width in points
Window state: 'normal', 'minimized', or 'maximized' (required for: set-state)

Command.Name: worksheetstyle
Command.Description: Worksheet styling operations for tab colors and visibility. Use sheet for lifecycle operations (create, rename, copy, delete, move). TAB COLORS: Use RGB values (0-255 each) to set custom tab colors for visual organization. VISIBILITY LEVELS: - 'visible': Normal visible sheet - 'hidden': Hidden but accessible via Format > Sheet > Unhide - 'veryhidden': Only accessible via VBA (protection against casual unhiding)

Option.Description: Blue color component (0-255) (required for: set-tab-color)
Green color component (0-255) (required for: set-tab-color)
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, decodes and saves as binary file.
Red color component (0-255) (required for: set-tab-color)
Session ID from 'session open' command
Name of the worksheet to color (required)
Visibility level: 'visible', 'hidden', or 'veryhidden' (required for: set-visibility)
